t: Add or change your profile photo
s: Set a photo on your account, change it or remove it.
x: You can add a profile photo to your account. It shows on your profile and in the user list. It is personal: each user manages their own photo. Open your profile (account menu > My profile), then tap the camera icon on the avatar. Camera icon: On the avatar; opens the options (change / remove the photo). 1. Tap the camera icon, then "Change photo". -> The gallery opens to pick an image. 2. Pick an image (JPEG, PNG or WEBP, 5 MB maximum). -> The photo is uploaded and shows immediately. (Check: To remove the photo: camera icon > "Remove photo".) First photo: You add your portrait: it replaces the initials shown by default. Without a photo, a circle with your initials is shown: this is normal. Unsupported image format - Cause: The file is not a valid JPEG, PNG or WEBP image. - Solution: Pick an image in one of these formats. Image too large - Cause: The image exceeds 5 MB. - Solution: Pick a lighter image (or reduce its size). A sharp, well-framed photo (face centered) makes the app look more professional.

s: Open a session with your e-mail and password, and choose the language.
k: connexion mot de passe e-mail langue
x: Signing in is the very first step: it opens your session and determines what you can do in the application, depending on your role (cashier, manager or administrator). Your administrator creates your account and gives you your e-mail address and password. The sign-in screen appears automatically when the application starts, as long as you are not signed in. Language selector (top right): Lets you choose Français, English or Deutsch. The change is immediate. E-mail field: Your work e-mail address. It must be valid (format name@domain). Password field: Your password (at least 6 characters). The eye icon shows or hides the typed text. "Forgot password" link: Opens the reset procedure (see the dedicated article). Sign-in button: Validates your credentials and opens your session. 1. If needed, choose your language at the top right. -> All texts immediately appear in the chosen language. 2. Enter your e-mail address in the first field. -> The field accepts input; a malformed address will be flagged on submit. 3. Enter your password in the second field. -> The text is hidden; tap the eye icon to check it. (Check: Check there is no extra space before submitting.) 4. Tap the sign-in button

s: Sign in without retyping your password, using fingerprint, face or Windows Hello.
k: biometrie empreinte visage Windows Hello connexion rapide
x: Biometric login saves you from typing your password every time: the device checks your fingerprint or face, then opens your session. Your credentials are stored only on THIS device, in its secure vault, and are used only after a successful biometric check. They are never sent anywhere else. This option only appears if your device has biometrics (fingerprint, face recognition or Windows Hello). On the web it is not available: use the password. On the sign-in screen: the "Enable biometric login" checkbox (below the password) and, once enabled, the "Sign in with biometrics" button. In Settings > Security > "Biometric login": to enable it (with password confirmation) or disable it. "Enable biometric login" checkbox (sign-in): Tick it before signing in to remember your credentials on this device. "Sign in with biometrics" button: Starts the check (fingerprint/face) then opens your session without a password. "Biometric login" switch (Settings > Security): Enables (after password confirmation) or disables biometrics. Disabling erases the stored credentials. 1. At sign-in, enter e-mail and password, tick "Enable biometric login", then submit. -> Your session opens and your credentials are

s: Receive a code by e-mail and set a new password, in 3 steps.
k: mot de passe oublie reinitialiser code
x: If you have forgotten your password, you can reset it yourself, without waiting for the administrator, using a code sent to your e-mail. On the sign-in screen, tap the "Forgot password" link below the password field. 1. Step 1: enter your e-mail and date of birth, then tap "Send code". -> A code is sent to your e-mail address. (Check: Check your mailbox (and the spam folder).) 2. Step 2: enter the received code, then tap "Verify". -> The code is validated and you move to the next step. 3. Step 3: enter your new password, then tap "Reset". -> The password is changed; you can sign in with the new one. The code has a limited validity. If it has expired, restart step 1 to receive a new one. Invalid or expired code - Cause: The entered code is wrong or no longer valid. - Solution: Restart step 1 to receive a new code, then re-enter it quickly. Copy and paste the code from the e-mail to avoid typos.

s: The home screen adapts to your role and groups your shortcuts by theme.
k: tableau de bord accueil menu navigation
x: After signing in, you land on a dashboard that differs by role: the cashier sees the sale screen, the manager and administrator see shortcut cards grouped by theme (Sales, Stock, etc.). The dashboard is the screen shown right after signing in. The top bar shows the company name and action icons (profile, notifications, help, logout depending on the role). Top bar (company header): Shows your company logo and name. Profile icon: Opens your profile (personal info, password). Help icon: Opens this Help Center. Logout icon: Closes your session (a confirmation is asked). 1. Spot the top bar and its icons. -> You identify your cross-cutting shortcuts (profile, help, logout). 2. Tap an action card (manager / admin) or use the sale screen (cashier). -> The matching screen opens. 3. To go back, use the back arrow at the top left. -> You return to the previous screen. The cards and icons shown depend on your role: if you do not see a feature, it is not allowed for your role. A stock alert banner may appear on the manager / admin dashboard: tap it to act directly.

t: Scan a barcode (scanner gun, camera or manual)
s: Understand the 3 ways to scan and the automatic scan as soon as you are on a flow screen.
x: Scanning instantly finds a product by its barcode, in sales, inventory and product search. It is much faster and more reliable than typing. Professional behavior: as soon as you are on a flow screen (Sales, Products, Movements), a scanner-gun scan is captured and processed AUTOMATICALLY, without pressing any button. Automatic (scanner-gun) scanning is active on the Sales, Products and Movements screens. A scan-icon button is also present there for the camera (mobile) and manual code entry. Scanner gun (USB / Bluetooth reader): Works on all devices. No button to press: you scan, the action triggers. Scan button (camera): Opens the camera to aim at a code (Android and web browser). On a Windows desktop without a camera, use a connected barcode reader or manual entry. Manual entry: Lets you type the code by hand, available on all devices. 1. Open the desired screen (Sales, Products or Movements). -> Scanner-gun scanning is immediately active (nothing to enable). 2. Scan the item with the scanner gun. -> The product is found and the screen action triggers (add to cart, open sheet, movement). A sound confirms. (Check: Check that the correct product was taken into account.) 3. Without a scanner gun: tap the scan button for the cam

t: Use the Help Center and search
s: Quickly find an answer: browse by category or search by keyword.
x: The Help Center explains, step by step, how to use the application. It only shows features available to your role, to avoid confusion. Tap the help icon (question mark) in the top bar of your dashboard. Search bar: Type a word (e.g. "sale", "invoice"): matching articles appear immediately. Category list: Groups articles by theme (Sales, Stock, etc.). 1. Open the Help Center via the help icon. -> The category list and the search bar appear. 2. Type a keyword, or tap a category then an article. -> The article opens with its sections (presentation, access, steps, examples, errors, tips). Use search rather than browsing: a single word is usually enough to find the right article.

s: View your personal information and change your password.
x: Your profile gathers your personal information (name, e-mail, company) and account actions, including changing your password. Tap the Profile icon (person) in the top bar. Personal information: First name, last name, e-mail, date of birth, ID card, company, member since. Change password: Starts the change procedure (via the secure reset). Logout: Closes your session (confirmation asked). 1. Open your profile via the Profile icon. -> Your information appears. 2. To change your password, tap "Change password" and follow the procedure. -> You receive a code and set a new password. (Check: Sign in again with the new password to confirm.) Editing some information may be reserved for the administrator. Change your password at first sign-in and never share it.

s: Self-register from the login screen: create the company, the admin account and choose a plan (free trial, no card).
k: inscription / sign up / Registrierung creer un compte / create account / Konto erstellen creer une entreprise / create company / Unternehmen erstellen abonnement essai / subscription trial / Abo Testphase verification email / email verification / E-Mail-Bestatigung
x: Sign-up lets a new user create their own company and admin account, with no intervention. It is the very first step to use the app: it creates your isolated workspace (tenant) and your administrator access. You start with a 14-day free trial, no credit card. You can choose and pay for a plan later from the Subscription screen. On the login screen, at the bottom, tap "Create a company". No prior account or login is required: it is a public flow. Company step: Company name, country (picker), number of employees, and optional fields (industry, address, tax number). Account step: First name, last name, email, date of birth (18+) and password (with live strength hints). Plan step: Plan choice (prices come from the server), monthly / yearly toggle, and currency based on country. Everything starts with a free trial. Review step: Summary of your information and acceptance of the terms of use before creation. Action: On the login screen, tap "Create a company". Result: The 4-step sign-up flow opens. Action: Fill in the company (name, country, number of employees), then tap Continue. Result: The Account step appears. Check: The country must be selected (it determines your billing currency).

t: Read the dashboard and daily indicators
s: Understand the key figures of the day and react to stock alerts.
k: tableau de bord indicateurs chiffre du jour alerte stock
x: The dashboard gives an immediate view of activity: sales of the day, number of transactions, and stock status (low / out). It helps run the day. It is the home screen of the manager and administrator, shown after signing in. Today overview: Total sales of the day, number of transactions and stock status. This block refreshes automatically when you return to the dashboard; a Refresh button (circular arrow icon, top right of the block) is still available. Low stock / Out of stock cards: Number of products below threshold or sold out; tappable to act. Stock alert banner: Appears when products are out of stock; tap it to see the list. Theme navigation cards: Grouped shortcuts (Sales, Stock, Settings...). 1. Check the daily overview to gauge activity. -> You see the sales and transactions of the day. 2. Tap a stock card (low / out) or the alert banner. -> You reach the affected products to restock. (Check: Check that critical products are handled.) Morning check: On arrival, you look at remaining sales and out-of-stock products to order. Automatic refresh: if you leave the app and come back, or close a screen opened on top of the dashboard, the daily overview reloads by itself. You no l

s: View refunds, filter them and see their details.
x: The refunds screen lists amounts returned to customers, with payment method, customer, original sale and who processed it. From the dashboard, tap the "Refunds" card. Payment method filter: Cash, card, mobile money, mixed, or all. Average refund: Summary indicator over the period. Details (original sale, processed by): Lets you trace back to the initial sale and the operator. 1. Open "Refunds" and filter by payment method if needed. -> The list and indicators update. 2. Tap a refund to see its original sale and details. -> The full detail appears. Refunding a sale is done from the sales history; this screen is mainly for follow-up and analysis. Watch the average refund and number of returns: a rise may signal a product problem.

s: Difference between refund (partial/full) and cancellation, and how to do each safely.
x: Two distinct operations. A REFUND returns part or all of the items: you pick items and quantities, the customer is refunded the exact amount paid (VAT INCLUDED), and the sale becomes "Partially refunded" or "Fully refunded". A CANCELLATION voids the WHOLE sale at once: all items return to stock and the sale becomes "Cancelled" (the linked invoice becomes "Cancelled"). Dashboard -> "Sales history". Tap a sale to open its details: the "Refund" and "Cancel" buttons appear depending on the sale state. "Refund" button: Shown while items remain to refund (completed or partially refunded sale). Opens the item selection. Refundable quantity: For each item, the quantity still refundable (already-refunded deducted). Fully refunded items no longer appear. "Cancel" button: Shown only on a completed, non-refunded sale. Voids the whole sale. 1. To REFUND: open the sale, tap "Refund", check items and adjust quantities (capped at refundable), choose the refund method, then confirm. -> A credit note is created, the amount (VAT included) is returned, stock is replenished and the sale status is updated. 2. To CANCEL: open the sale, tap "Cancel", enter a reason, then confirm. -> The sale becomes "Canc

s: Build the cart, choose the customer, take the payment and deliver the invoice.
k: vente caisse panier paiement encaisser facture monnaie
x: The sale screen is the heart of the cashier work: it records sold items, takes the payment and delivers an invoice to the customer. Each sale updates stock automatically and feeds the history, reports and accounting. The cashier lands directly on the sale screen after signing in. The administrator opens it via the "Sales" card of the dashboard. Products / scan area: Lets you add items to the cart: automatic scanner-gun, scan button (camera), or search/selection. Categories (filter + search): List of categories to filter the displayed products. A search bar at the top quickly finds a category by name (useful when there are many); tap "All categories" to remove the filter. Cart: List of items with quantity and price. You can adjust quantities or remove an item. Customer (optional): Lets you attach the sale to a customer (useful for the e-mailed invoice and loyalty). Loyalty points: If loyalty is enabled and a customer is selected, you can use their points as a discount. Totals (subtotal excl. tax, tax, total incl. tax): Summarize the amount due; updated automatically. "Proceed to payment" and "Save as quote" buttons: Proceed to payment opens the payment; Save as quote sets the cart a

s: Find a sale, see its details, cancel or refund it, export the list.
x: The history lists the sales made, with their amount, customer and status. It is used to verify, find an invoice, fix a mistake (cancellation) or refund. Tap the History icon in the top bar (cashier) or the "Sales history" card on the dashboard (manager / admin). Status filters (all / completed / cancelled): Narrow the displayed list. Filter by seller (admin / manager): For admin and manager only: a "Filter by seller" dropdown shows only a specific cashier's sales (or "All sellers"). A cashier only ever sees their own sales - this menu does not appear for them, and the server guarantees they cannot see others'. Sale details: Shows the items, customer, payment method, change given, date. Cancel / Refund: Lets you cancel a sale (with a reason) or refund it. Export: Exports the list (according to rights) for archiving or analysis. 1. Open the history and filter by status if needed. -> The list updates. 2. Tap a sale to see its details. -> The full detail appears. 3. To fix: use Cancel (enter a reason) or Refund, then confirm. -> The sale status is updated accordingly. (Check: Check that the status changed to cancelled / refunded.) [Simple] Find a sale of the day: You open the history,

s: Set a cart aside as a quote, then convert it into a sale later.
x: A quote is a priced proposal, without payment or stock movement. Handy when the customer is still deciding or will pay later. From the sale screen, build the cart then tap "Save as quote". To find quotes, open the Quotes screen (cashier quote icon). Quote list with status: Possible statuses: draft, sent, accepted, converted, expired, cancelled. Convert to sale: Turns the quote into a sale to be paid. Download PDF / Send by e-mail: Delivers the quote to the customer. Delete: Deletes a non-converted quote. 1. On the sale screen, add the items then tap "Save as quote". -> The quote is created (no payment, stock unchanged). 2. Later, open the Quotes screen and tap "Convert to sale" on the right quote. -> A sale is prepared from the quote; you take payment normally. [Simple] Quote then sale: The customer wants to think: you save the cart as a quote. They return the next day, you convert and take payment. A converted quote can no longer be deleted; it is linked to the created sale. Attach a customer to the quote so you can e-mail it and track its acceptance.

s: Track stock status, recent movements and access inventory actions.
x: The inventory view gathers the stock essentials: number of products, distribution by category, low / out-of-stock / soon-to-expire products, and recent movements. From the dashboard, tap the "Manage stock" card. Counters (products, categories): Total managed volumes. Low / Out of stock / Expiring soon: Alert indicators to watch. Distribution by category (donut chart): Interactive ring: each slice is a category, sized by its number of products. Tap a slice, or a row in the legend on the right, to highlight it; the ring center then shows the category name, product count and percentage. When there are many categories, the smallest are grouped into an "Others" slice so the chart stays readable; the legend still lists every category and scrolls. Trends (stock value, product count, out-of-stock): Three day-by-day curves below the counters: total stock value, product count and number of out-of-stock items. WHAT IT IS FOR: the counters at the top show TODAY's situation (a photo); the curves show the EVOLUTION over time (a film), to steer your stock - for example spotting a stock value that keeps rising (too much cash tied up in goods), out-of-stock counts rising week after week (lost sales

s: Organize products into categories to better find and analyze them.
x: Categories group products by family (e.g. drinks, hygiene). They ease search, filtering and the distribution shown in the inventory view. From the inventory view ("Manage stock"), open the categories. Category list + search: Browse and find a category. Category details: Information (created by, created on) and associated products. Delete a category: Deletes the category after confirmation. 1. Open categories from the inventory view. -> The category list appears. 2. Tap a category for its details, or delete one (confirmation required). -> The detail appears, or the category is deleted. (Check: Before deletion, check no important product depends on it.) Organize drinks: You group your drinks into a dedicated category to filter them quickly in inventory. Error while deleting: Cause: The category could not be deleted (e.g. still in use). Solution: Reassign the products to another category first, then retry. Keep few, clear categories: too many makes classification confusing.

s: Spot products near expiry or expired to act in time.
x: This screen lists products whose expiry date is approaching or passed, to sell them first or remove them. Automatic alerts: managers and administrators are notified on their own (in-app notification + e-mail) when a product is nearing expiry. The company default delay (days before expiry, default 7) is set in Settings > Company > "Expiry alerts". Anyone can enable/disable these e-mails in Settings > Notification preferences ("Products expiring soon"). Already-expired products are flagged as urgent (red). Scheduled expiry digest: on top of real-time alerts, each user can schedule a dedicated e-mail summary in Settings > Notification preferences > "Expiring products digest". There you choose the days of the week, the hour, the timezone and above all the alert lead time (products expiring within X days, e.g. 7, 10 or 15 days, and those already expired). This digest is independent from the low-stock daily digest. From the inventory view, open "Expiring soon" (or "See all" on the expiry alert). Filter by number of days: Show what expires within X days. Sort by expiry date: From most urgent to furthest (or reverse). Status (expiring soon / expired) and stock: Identifies urgency and the q

s: Align theoretical stock with the real stock after a count, loss or donation.
k: ajustement inventaire physique perte don ecart
x: Inventory adjustment corrects the recorded stock to match reality (after a physical count, loss, breakage or donation), with a traced reason. From the inventory view ("Manage stock"), open "Inventory adjustment". Products to adjust (search / scan): Add the products whose quantity must be corrected. Physical quantity + reason: Enter the actually counted quantity and the reason (loss, donation, other); optional batch number. 1. Open "Inventory adjustment" and add the products to correct. -> The selected products appear in the adjustment list. 2. For each product, enter the real physical quantity and the reason. -> The gap between theoretical and real is taken into account. 3. Confirm the adjustment. -> Stock is updated and the operation is traced with its reason. (Check: Check the new quantities after confirmation.) After a physical count: The count gives 18 units while the system shows 20: you enter 18, reason "loss", and stock is corrected. Adjustment is for exceptional corrections; routine entries/exits go through stock movements. Always give a precise reason (loss, donation, breakage): it stays traced for audit and accounting.

s: Create, edit and find products; attach a barcode for scanning.
x: Products are the basis of stock and sales. Each product has a price, a quantity and can carry a barcode that makes it instantly scannable. From the inventory view ("Manage stock") or the Products screen. Scanning an unknown code there opens creation directly, with the code pre-filled. Search and filters (low stock / out of stock): Find a product or target alerts. Create / Edit a product: Name, price, quantity, category, barcode, thresholds, expiry. Scan (scanner gun / camera): Known scan: opens the record. Unknown scan: opens creation with the code pre-filled. Action: Open the Products screen. To create, tap "+" (or scan an unknown code). Result: The product form opens (code pre-filled if scanned). Action: Fill in name, price, quantity, category and, if possible, the barcode; save. Result: The product is created and becomes scannable if it has a barcode. Check: Scan the product to verify it is recognized. [simple] Create a product by scanning it: On the Products screen, you scan a new item: the form opens with the code pre-filled, you enter name and price, you confirm. A product created without a barcode will not be scannable until you attach one. Set the alert thresholds (low stoc

s: Trace each stock entry and exit, and view the movements history.
x: Stock movements record all variations: entries (receptions, corrections) and exits (sales, losses). They ensure a reliable and traceable stock. Administrator dashboard, Stock section, "Stock movements" card (or from the inventory view). Movement activity (chart): Above the list, a bar chart compares entries (green) and exits (orange) over three periods: today, the last 7 days and the last 30 days. The exact value is shown above each bar, with an Entries / Exits legend. Figures come from the server (real count, not a sample) and update after each new movement. Movements list: Entries / exits with product, quantity and date. New movement (scan possible): Choose the product (or scan it), the type (entry/exit) and the quantity. Clickable cards (today entries / exits, total): At the top, three cards show EXACT counts (computed by the server, not a sample): entries today, exits today, total movements. Each card is CLICKABLE: tapping "entries today" filters the list to today's entries, "exits today" to today's exits, and "total movements" resets the filters (show all). Filters and sorting: The bar lets you search (reference, product name or reason), sort (date or quantity, asc/desc) and o

t: Import data: products, customers and stock (CSV)
s: Import or update your product catalog, your customer file AND your stock from a file, without re-keying or duplicates.
k: import / import / Import csv / csv / csv migration / migration / Migration catalogue / catalog / Katalog clients / customers / Kunden stock / stock / Bestand inventaire / inventory / Inventur
x: Import lets you add or update lots of data at once from a CSV file (a spreadsheet you can export from Excel, Google Sheets or LibreOffice). The screen has three tabs: Products, Customers and Stock (the latter with two sub-tabs: Inventory levels and Movements). Ideal for onboarding or bulk fixing. The server validates each row: valid rows are imported, rows in error are listed with a clear explanation - nothing is ever silently lost. Administrator dashboard, Stock section, "Import data" card, then choose the Products or Customers tab. (Administrators only.) "Products", "Customers" and "Stock" tabs: At the top, three tabs. Products imports your catalog; Customers imports your customer file; Stock updates the quantities of EXISTING products, with two sub-tabs: "Inventory levels" (you give the new total stock after a count) and "Movements" (you record entries/exits). Each tab works the same way (download the template, fill it, import it) and keeps its report when you switch tabs. Stock columns (Stock tab): The product is identified by its 'Reference' (or 'Name'): it must ALREADY exist (stock import never creates a product; use the Products tab to create). Levels sub-tab: 'Quantity' = t

s: Register a new customer for billing, loyalty and follow-up.
x: Registering a customer lets you address invoices to them, accumulate loyalty points and find their history. Tap the "New customer" icon (cashier top bar) or the matching card on the admin dashboard. First name and Last name: Customer identity (required). Phone: Contact number (required, valid format). E-mail: Enables sending invoices by e-mail. Address and Country: Customer postal details. Save customer: Validates the creation. 1. Open "New customer". -> The customer form appears. 2. Fill in first name, last name, phone, and ideally e-mail and address. -> The required fields are validated along the way. (Check: Check the phone number (format).) 3. Tap "Save customer". -> The customer is created and available for selection in a sale. [Simple] Quick customer at the till: A customer asks for an invoice in their name: you add them (first name, last name, phone, e-mail) then select them on the sale. Invalid phone number: (Cause) The entered number does not match a valid format. (Solution) Enter a correct number (prefix + digits, no stray characters). Missing required fields: (Cause) A required field (first name, last name, phone) is empty. (Solution) Complete all required fields before

s: See a customer points balance and the history of earnings/redemptions.
x: The loyalty program accumulates points for the customer over their purchases. These points can then be used as a discount during a sale. When selecting a customer (in a sale), open the loyalty record of the chosen customer. Points balance: Number of points currently available for the customer. Points earned / redeemed (totals): Two cards summarize the lifetime total of points earned (green) and redeemed (red) by the customer. Loyalty history: Points movements: earnings, redemptions (discounts) and reversals. 1. In a sale, select the customer then open their loyalty. -> The balance and points history appear. 2. To use points: return to the sale and apply them as a discount (see "Process a sale"). -> The loyalty discount reduces the total due. [Simple] Check the points: A customer asks how many points they have: you select them and read the balance in the loyalty record. Loyalty is available only if it is enabled in the company settings. Always select the customer BEFORE finalizing the sale so they accumulate (or use) their points.

t: View all loyal customers and their points
s: The list of all your customers with their points balance, ranked from most to least loyal.
x: This screen shows ALL your customers and how many loyalty points each one has. At the top, a company summary: number of customers with points, total points in circulation and their value in currency. From the home screen, open the "Customer loyalty" card (Sales and customers section). It only appears if the loyalty program is enabled in the company settings. Statistics header: Three figures: loyal customers (with points), points in circulation, total value in currency. Search and sort: Search a customer by name; sort by Ranking (points) or by Name. Customer row: The rank (in points sort), the name, the value in currency, and a badge with the number of points. 1. Open "Customer loyalty" from the home screen. -> The list appears, ranked by points (the most loyal first). 2. Tap a customer. -> Their loyalty record opens (balance + points history). [Simple] Find your best customers: Keep the sort on "Ranking (points)": the customers with the most points appear at the top of the list. The card and screen are only visible if loyalty is enabled in the company settings. Customers without points appear with 0 points. Use search to quickly find a specific customer, or sort by Name to browse a

s: Find issued invoices, open and print them as PDF.
x: The invoices screen lists the documents issued during sales. Each invoice can be opened and printed as PDF. From the dashboard, tap the "Invoices" card. Invoice list: Issued invoices, with a display filter. Details / PDF: Opens the invoice to view and print it. 1. Open "Invoices". -> The invoice list appears. 2. Tap an invoice to open it, then print or export the PDF. -> The invoice appears in printable format. If no invoice appears, no billed sale has been recorded yet for the period. For an invoice to be e-mailed, the customer must have an e-mail address set.

s: Choose a plan, pay by card or Mobile Money (Orange Money, MTN, M-Pesa), manage renewal, cancel or change plan.
k: abonnement paiement orange money mobile money carte renouvellement
x: The Subscription screen lets the administrator manage the company plan (Basic, Professional, Enterprise), pay online and track the due date. Payment happens on a secure page: by bank card or by Mobile Money (Orange Money, MTN MoMo, M-Pesa) depending on your country. When your company is created, you automatically get a 14-day free trial. The countdown starts at your first login. A banner at the top of the dashboard always reminds you how many days are left. No card is required during the trial. From the dashboard, tap the "Subscription" card. Only the administrator can pay or change the subscription. Trial banner: Shown at the top of the dashboard during the free trial. It shows the days left and changes colour as the end approaches (green, then orange, then red). Tap it to open the Subscription screen. Current plan card: Shows your plan, status, due date and renewal mode (card = automatic, Mobile Money = by reminder). Automatic renewal: A switch enables/disables renewal. Turning it off schedules cancellation at the end of the period. Plan cards: Each plan has a button to activate it (first payment) or change plan (upgrade/downgrade). Monthly / Yearly: A selector switches between m

s: Issue an invoice directly without going through a POS sale: enter items, customer and options, then confirm.
x: Allows issuing an invoice outside the POS flow (services, deferred billing). The created invoice is in DRAFT. The server fills in company info automatically. Dashboard > Sales/Billing section > 'New Invoice' tile. Permission: invoices.manage. Customer section: Customer picker by name + manual fields (name, address, email). All optional. Items: Editable lines: name, quantity, price ex-VAT, discount %, VAT %. Totals updated in real time. 1. Dashboard > 'New Invoice'. -> Creation screen opened. 2. Optional: select a customer. Add items (name, quantity, price ex-VAT, VAT). Choose currency. -> The summary updates in real time. 3. Tap 'Create invoice'. -> DRAFT invoice created, redirected to list. If the global discount exceeds the total inc-VAT, the server rejects creation. Correct the discount. Add at least one item: Cause: No line entered. Solution: Tap 'Add item'. Configure default payment conditions in settings to save time.

s: Track received payments, filter, sort and see averages.
k: transactions / transactions / Transaktionen paiements / payments / Zahlungen analyse / analysis / Analyse
x: The transactions dashboard shows received payments, with indicators (average amount per transaction) and filters by period, amount and payment method. IMPORTANT - a SALE and a TRANSACTION are not the same. A sale can be settled by SEVERAL payments (e.g. part in cash, part by card): each payment counts as ONE transaction. The number of transactions is therefore always GREATER THAN OR EQUAL to the number of sales. Concrete example: 150 sales of which 60 paid in 2 parts = 210 transactions. This is not an error: the "Today's overview" screen counts SALES (150), while this dashboard counts payment TRANSACTIONS (210). From the dashboard, Finance section, "Transactions" card. Average amount per transaction: Summary indicator of activity. Distribution by payment method (donut): In the Overview tab, a donut chart shows the share of each payment method (cash, card, transfer...). The percentage is shown directly inside each slice and the period total in the center. Tap a slice to see, in the center, the related method and its percentage. A legend beside it details each method with its amount and percentage. Filters and sorting: By period, amount range, payment method; sort by amount. Action:

s: Create and find the suppliers you buy from.
x: Suppliers are the companies you source from. Registering them lets you attach purchases and track receptions. A supplier is never used alone: it is always attached to a purchase. It is through the supplier purchase that your stock is replenished, the cost actually paid enters the margin calculation, and the VAT paid is recorded as deductible VAT. The supplier is therefore the starting point of your whole supply chain. Administrator dashboard, Purchasing section, "Suppliers" card. Search, filters (active / inactive) and sorting: Quickly find a supplier. Add a supplier: Create a supplier record (contact details). Action: Open "Suppliers" then tap "Add a supplier". Result: The supplier form opens. Action: Fill in the details and save. Result: The supplier is available when creating a purchase. Deactivate a supplier you no longer use rather than deleting it, to keep the purchase history.

s: Enter a supplier purchase; the reception updates stock, cost (weighted average) and deductible VAT.
x: A purchase records what you order and receive from a supplier. It is the entry point of your goods into the application. Key point to understand: a purchase does NOT add a new product, it adds STOCK to a product that already exists in "Products". So you create the product once, then buy it as many times as needed, from any supplier. When you receive the purchase, three things happen automatically: (1) product stock increases; (2) the cost actually paid feeds the weighted average cost, which is then used to compute your margin; (3) the VAT paid to the supplier is recorded as deductible VAT (it is deducted from the VAT you collect on your sales). Purchase life cycle: it is first created as a "Draft" (you can still edit it), then set to "Received" when the goods arrive. It is this switch to "Received" that triggers the stock entry and the calculations above. Administrator dashboard, Purchasing section, "Purchases" card. Purchase list with status: Filters by supplier, period and status (e.g. draft). New purchase: Enter a purchase (supplier, products, quantities, prices). Product field (search by name): On each line, tap the product field: a search window opens. Type the product name an

s: Track revenue, margin, VAT and treasury; produce periodic reports.
k: comptabilité chiffre d affaires marge tva trésorerie rapport
x: The accounting module consolidates the financials: net revenue, cost of goods sold (COGS), gross margin, income statement, VAT, treasury and stock value. It also produces detailed periodic reports. Administrator dashboard, Finance section, "Accounting" card. Reports and settings are reachable from this module. Financial overview (net revenue excl. VAT, COGS, gross margin): Key indicators over the chosen period. The net revenue shown is net EXCLUDING VAT: this is the legal basis for the income statement (HGB §275). It differs from total cash received (incl. VAT). Income statement / Treasury / Stock value: Consolidated views of financial health. Trends (margin / cash flow / VAT charts): Three day-by-day curves below the indicators: gross margin, treasury (cash in minus cash out balance) and net collected VAT. They follow the chosen period, load in the background (the indicators stay visible right away) and can dip below zero on a loss-making day. Hover a point to read the exact date and amount. Reports and settings: Detailed periodic reports; settings (fiscal year, VAT, currency). Action: Open "Accounting" and choose the period. Result: The financial overview is computed for that per

s: What VAT is, the difference between collected, deductible and due VAT, and why several rates exist.
x: VAT (value added tax) is a tax you collect FOR the state. This money is not yours: you collect it on your sales, you pay it on your purchases, and you remit the difference to the state. Three notions to remember: (1) COLLECTED VAT, the one you charge your customers on your SALES; (2) DEDUCTIBLE VAT, the one your suppliers charge you on your PURCHASES; (3) VAT DUE (to remit) = collected VAT minus deductible VAT. Only this difference is what you actually pay to the state. Why SEVERAL rates: the law sets different rates depending on the product type (for example a standard rate, and a reduced rate for food). The same business can therefore have several rates at once. That is why the rate is chosen PER LINE (on each sale and each purchase), not once for the whole company. The default VAT is set in Settings > Company ("Default VAT" field). The VAT summary (collected, deductible, due) is available in the Accounting section. Default VAT (Settings > Company): The rate proposed automatically on each new purchase line. Set it to the rate you use most often. Per-line VAT rate: On each purchase (and sale) line, an editable percentage field. It starts from the default VAT but stays adjustable i

t: Accounting: what each term means and how each amount is computed
s: The meaning of every accounting term AND the exact formula behind each figure. Full transparency.
k: formule calcul / formula calculation / Formel Berechnung CA chiffre d affaires / revenue turnover / Umsatz marge / margin / Marge TVA / VAT / MwSt COGS cout / COGS cost / COGS Kosten tresorerie / treasury / Liquiditaet valeur stock / stock value / Bestandswert glossaire / glossary / Glossar
x: This article explains the MEANING of every accounting term and the exact FORMULA of each displayed amount. Goal: you should never wonder where a figure comes from. Golden rule: no calculation is done in the app. All amounts are computed by the server (single source of truth) from your real sales, purchases and stock. Gross revenue: Total incl. tax taken on the period sales (before returns). Formula: sum of sale amounts. Credit notes (excl. VAT): Total net amount refunded on customer returns of the period. Formula: sum of credit note lines (net amount refunded per item). This is deducted from gross net revenue to get net revenue excl. VAT. Net revenue excl. VAT: Real revenue after returns, EXCLUDING VAT. This is the income statement figure (GuV, HGB §275). Formula: net revenue excl. VAT = gross excl. VAT - credit notes excl. VAT. Do not confuse with net revenue incl. VAT (which includes collected VAT - not used in the P&L). Excl. tax (net): Sales amount without VAT. Formula: sum of sale net subtotals. It is the margin base. Incl. tax (gross): What the customer pays. Formula: incl. tax = excl. tax + VAT. COGS (cost of goods sold): Purchase cost of the items actually sold. Formula: su

s: Create, search, edit and deactivate accounts; understand roles.

x: User management lets you create your team accounts and assign each a role: cashier, manager or administrator. The role determines what each can do. Cashier: sales and till. Manager: monitoring, stock, reports. Administrator: full access (users, settings, accounting, oversight). Administrator dashboard, Administration section, "Manage users" card. Search and filters (active / inactive): Find a user and filter by status. Create a user: Enter first name, last name, e-mail, password (confirmed), date of birth, ID card, and choose the role. Edit / Deactivate: Update an account or deactivate it (with a reason) instead of deleting it. Action: Open "Manage users". Result: The user list appears. Action: To create: tap Create, fill the form and choose the role. Result: The account is created; the user can sign in. Check: Check the assigned role (it governs access). Action: To remove access: open the user and deactivate them (enter a reason). Result: The account can no longer sign in, but the history is kept. [simple] New cashier: You hire a cashier: you create their account with the Cashier role and give them their credentials. [intermediate] Employee leaving: An employee leaves: you deactiv

s: Grant or revoke specific rights for a person, on top of their role.
k: droits / permissions / Rechte accorder / grant / gewaehren retirer / revoke / entziehen autorisation / authorization / Berechtigung matrice droits utilisateur / user permission matrix / Benutzerrechte-Matrix
x: Each role (cashier, manager, administrator) already grants a set of default rights. Fine-grained permissions let you ADDITIONALLY tailor those rights PERSON BY PERSON: grant a right the role lacks, or revoke a right the role has. Examples: allow one specific manager to edit or delete products; allow a trusted cashier to issue refunds; or conversely remove refunds from a cashier. Changes affect ONLY the chosen person, never other users with the same role. The server remains the sole authority on access: even if an option appears, an unauthorized action is refused server-side. Administrator dashboard > "Manage users" > tap a user, then the shield icon "Manage permissions" at the top right. This icon only appears for administrators. Matrix by module: Rights are grouped by module (Products, Sales, Stock, Customers, Invoices, Reports, etc.). Each row is a specific action (View, Create, Edit, Delete, Refund...). Checkbox: Tick to grant the right, untick to remove it. A label shows whether the right is "included by default in the role". Save: Applies the changes. The server automatically computes additions and removals relative to the role. Action: Open "Manage users" and tap the person c

x: When you create an employee, BizCoRa automatically chooses the onboarding method based on whether you provided an email address. Option A - Email provided: an activation email is sent automatically. The employee clicks the link in the email, sets their own password and their account is activated. Option B - No email: the account is created active with a temporary password. This password is shown ONLY ONCE: note it and share it privately with the employee. On first login, they will be required to set a new personal password. Administrator dashboard > Administration > Manage users > "New user" button (+ icon at top right of the list). First name field: Employee first name (required). Last name field: Employee last name (required). Email field (optional): If you fill in the email, BizCoRa automatically sends an activation link. If you leave this field empty, a temporary password is generated. ID card number: Employee legal identifier (required). Birth date: Select the date via the calendar (required). Role: Cashier, Manager or Administrator. Defines the default permissions. Action: From the user list, tap the "+" button at the top right. Result: The creation form opens. Action: Enter

s: Understand why the app may sign you out (session expired, account deactivated, inactivity), how to stay signed in, and how to set the inactivity delay.
k: déconnexion session inactivité rester connecté sécurité reconnexion
x: To protect your data, the app may end your session in three cases: (1) your session expired (after a long period), (2) an administrator deactivated your account, (3) you stayed INACTIVE too long (no action). In every case you are returned to the sign-in screen with a message explaining why. Inactivity sign-out matters on a shared terminal (a till): if you walk away without signing out, nobody can act in your place. Before signing you out, the app warns you with a countdown and lets you choose to stay signed in. The inactivity delay is set in Settings > Security > "Automatic sign-out". The inactivity warning appears on its own when you stay idle. The message explaining a sign-out is shown on the sign-in screen. Inactivity warning: A "Still there?" dialog with a countdown. Two buttons: "Stay signed in" (keeps the session) and "Sign out". If the countdown reaches zero, you are signed out automatically. Reason message (sign-in screen): A banner on the sign-in screen tells you why the session ended: session expired, account deactivated, or inactivity. "Automatic sign-out" selector: In Settings > Security: a menu to choose the inactivity delay (5, 10, 15, 30 or 60 minutes), or "Never" to

s: Understand why "daily" reports follow the company timezone (not your device), and how to set it correctly.
k: fuseau horaire heure date rapport du jour voyage
x: IMPORTANT. Each sale is recorded with a universal time (UTC), a single reference valid worldwide. But to say "TODAY's sales", you must know when the day starts and ends - which depends on a timezone. The reference used is YOUR COMPANY timezone (where you operate), NOT your phone's. This is intentional: wherever you are (travelling, abroad), "today" stays your company's day. So the figures never change depending on where you view the app from. The company time is shown at the top of the dashboard ("Business time" banner with date, time and timezone). The timezone is set in Settings > Company (administrator). "Business time" banner: A live clock showing the date and time in the company timezone, plus the timezone name (e.g. Africa/Douala) and offset (e.g. UTC+01:00). Timezone setting (admin): In Settings > Company: a dropdown lists timezones (e.g. Douala UTC+1, Paris UTC+1/+2...). Set it to the country where the company operates. 1. Administrator: open Settings > Company, find "Timezone" and select your country (e.g. Douala (UTC+1) for Cameroon), then save. -> All "daily" reports and the displayed clock now follow this timezone. (Check: Check that the banner at the top of the dashboa

Account / Profile: Access to your information and password. Company and stock thresholds (per rights): Company settings and alert thresholds, available depending on the role. 1. Open settings. -> The different sections appear. 2. Change the language or enable dark mode in the General section. -> The change applies immediately to the whole app. (Check: Check that the display matches your choice.) Company settings (logo, legal identity) are generally reserved for the administrator. The language and theme choice is personal to your session and does not affect other users.

Appearance / Logo: Logo and shape (round / square) applied to documents and the app. Legal identity and bank details: Share capital, tax ID, bank; appear on official documents. s: Track who did what, when and from where; filter by period and source.

x: The audit log records important actions: who (author), what (action), on which element (entity), when, from which IP address. It is essential for security and control. Administrator dashboard, Administration section, "Audit log" card. Reserved for the administrator. Filters (period, source): Narrow by date and source (accounts, sales). Columns (author, action, entity, IP, changes): Details of each recorded event. Action: Open the audit log. Result: The list of events appears. [intermediate] Verify a sensitive operation: A sale was cancelled: you filter on the period and the Sales source to see who cancelled it and when. If access is denied, your role is not administrator: only the admin sees the audit log. Check the log in case of an incident (cash discrepancy, deletion) to quickly identify the origin.

t: Push notifications (even when the app is closed)
s: Get important alerts on your device even when the app is closed (web and installed app).
x: In addition to real-time notifications (when the app is open), you can receive PUSH notifications directly on your device - even if the app is closed or you are logged out. Just like a banking app: you are alerted right away, and the detail appears once you log in. For security, the pushed notification stays GENERIC: it shows no sensitive data (no product name, no figure). You must open the app and log in to see the content - so even on a locked or shared phone, nothing leaks. Available on the WEB (browser) and as an app installed to your home screen (PWA), on desktop and mobile. Open the notification center (bell icon): an "Enable notifications" banner appears until it is done. "Enable notifications" banner: A button that asks the browser for permission then subscribes you to push. It only appears on web/PWA, until you accept or decline. "Later" hides it without enabling anything. 1. Open the notification center (bell icon). If the "Enable notifications" banner is present, tap "Enable". -> The browser asks for permission to show notifications. (Check: Accept. If you decline, you can re-enable later in your browser site settings (permissions).) 2. On mobile, for better notification

s: Receive alerts (low stock, out of stock, system), read them and set their preferences.
x: Notifications keep you informed of important events: low stock, out of stock, and system messages. They prevent bad surprises (unseen stock-out). They arrive in real time: as soon as an event happens, the alert appears (and the sound plays if enabled), whatever screen you are on. If the connection is briefly lost, missed alerts are caught up automatically once it is restored: no notification is lost. The notification center opens via the bell icon at the top of the dashboard (manager / admin). Preferences are available to everyone via Settings > Notification preferences. Filters (unread / all, by type): Show unread or the whole history; filter by type (stock, system). Mark as read / Mark all as read: Manage what has been seen. Preferences (alert types + e-mail): Enable e-mail reception per type (critical stock, low stock, daily digest); in-app is always active. Notification sound: A sound plays when a new notification arrives, whatever screen you are on in the app. You can turn it on or off in Settings > General > "Notification sound"; a "Test" button (play icon) next to it lets you hear the sound at any time. This setting is specific to this device. Daily digest (days, time, timez

s: Ask business questions in French (or English / German) and receive answers based on your company's real data.
k: assistant ia intelligence artificielle question chat stock ventes clients
x: The AI Assistant lets you ask questions about your business in natural language, without navigating menus or building reports manually. It answers only from your company's real data (products, sales, customers, stock movements): it cannot access another company's data. Each answer may include the list of sources used (data table concerned), letting you verify where the information comes from. From the dashboard, scroll to the "Artificial Intelligence" section, then tap the "AI Assistant" card. Message area: Shows the current conversation. Blue bubbles (right) are your questions; grey bubbles (left) are the assistant's answers. Suggestion chips: Shown when the conversation is empty: suggest typical questions to tap directly to get started. Input field: At the bottom of the screen. Type your question then tap the send arrow (or press Enter). "Thinking" indicator: Three animated dots appear while the assistant prepares its answer. Sending is disabled during this time. Source chips: Shown below each assistant answer. Indicate the data tables consulted (e.g. Products, Sales, Customers). "Clear" button: Top right, available when the conversation contains messages. Lets you start fresh. 1
